title: ammobium craspedioides
intro: Ammobium craspedioides, also known as woolly heads, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, often made into teas or infusions, to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, providing relief from congestion and inflammation. Additionally, Ammobium craspedioides is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help to prevent the spread of infections and promote overall health and well-being.
